status_detail: Coordination of multiple small-scale (0.5-5 MW) decentralized green hydrogen production facilities across French industrial territories. Supported by the 'Territoires d'Industrie' French government program. Includes sites in Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes, Grand Est, and Hauts-de-France. PEM electrolysis serving local industrial and mobility needs.

data_management.narrative_summary: The French 'Territoires d'Industrie' program has supported the deployment of small-to-medium-scale (0.5-5 MW) decentralized green hydrogen production facilities across multiple French industrial regions, including Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes, Grand Est, and Hauts-de-France. These facilities use PEM electrolysis to produce green hydrogen for local industrial and mobility applications, demonstrating the viability of distributed hydrogen production models. The program, backed by the French government's 'France 2030' investment plan, has created a network of operational hydrogen production sites that serve as a model for decentralized industrial decarbonization. These facilities complement France's larger centralized projects like Normand'Hy (GA-PR-001) and H2V Dunkerque (GA-PR-015).

power.grid_connection_detail: Multiple grid connections at diverse French industrial territories

offtake.offtaker_name: Local industrial customers and hydrogen mobility networks across multiple French regions

sources.title: France's decentralized hydrogen strategy — Territoires d'Industrie program
sources.author: French Ministry of Industry / Hydrogen Europe
